preamble: The shift from the voiced alveolar fricative [z] to the voiced velar fricative [ɣ] is a process of backing or retraction. This transformation is relatively rare but occurs in specific phonotactic environments or as part of systemic consonant shifts in languages with high dorsal activity.

examples.note: Retraction of sibilants in certain morphophonemic environments or historical developments.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Colarusso, J. (1988). The Northwest Caucasian Languages: A Phonological Survey.
